title: Supply
description: Supplies are transactions of telecommunications, broadcasting and electronic (TBE) services. To document pieces of evidence for the place of supply, you can attach [evidence objects](https://vatstack.com/docs/evidences) to a supply object. Supplies are also used to automatically generate a quarterly tax report with currency conversion at official exchange rates.

country_code.description: 2-letter ISO country code of the place of supply that is relevant for the `vat.rate`.

created.description: ISO date at which the object was created.

currency.description: 3-letter ISO 4217 currency code used to charge the `amount`.

description.description: An arbitrary string to describe the supplied item. Often useful for displaying to users.

evidence.description: Populated evidence object if an ID is attached. You can attach an evidence object with the `evidence` body parameter in the POST request. Defaults to `null`. See [evidence object](https://vatstack.com/docs/evidences) for reference.

evidence_status.description: Status of whether the attached evidence object sufficiently proves the place of supply established in `country_code`. Will be either `sufficient` or `insufficient`.

invoice_number.description: A custom string for the invoice number issued to the customer. It’s advisable to follow sequential numbering.

issued.description: ISO date at which `invoice_number` was issued.

name.description: A custom string for the name of the customer.

notes.description: A custom string for additional notes.

updated.description: ISO date at which the object was updated.

validation.description: Populated validation object if an ID is attached. You can attach a validation object with the `validation` body parameter in the POST request. Defaults to `null`. See [validation object](https://vatstack.com/docs/validations) for reference.

inclusive.description: Specifies if the `amount_total` is inclusive (common for EU consumers) or exclusive of VAT. This affects how the `vat.amount` is calculated.

rate.description: VAT rate applicable for the place of supply established in `country_code`.

rate_type.description: Automatically determined type of VAT rate based on inputs. Can be `null`, `exempt`, `reduced`, `reverse_charge`, `standard` or `zero`.
